2605.05929.title: Which Are the Low-Resource Languages of the Semantic Web?
2605.05929.paper_summary: Emerging digital technologies are exacerbating the existing divide in Open Access Data (OAD) between high-and low-resource languages, excluding many communities from the global digital transformation. Multilingual Linked Open Data Knowledge Graphs (LOD KGs) could contribute to mitigating this divide through cross-lingual transfer; however, no clear quantitative definition of low-resource languages has yet been established in the context of LOD KGs. In this poster, we present a methodology to analyze the distribution of languages across LOD KGs and propose a preliminary multi-level categorization based on DBpedia, BabelNet, and Wikidata. This categorization is leveraged to bring a formal definition of low-, high-, and medium-resource languages that could be later leveraged to select cross-lingual transfer candidates.
2605.05929.paper_summary_zh: 新興的數位技術正在加劇高資源語言和低資源語言之間的開放獲取數據 (OAD) 的現有鴻溝，使許多社群被排除在全球數位轉型之外。多語言鏈接開放數據知識圖譜 (LOD KGs) 可以通過跨語言轉移來幫助減輕這一鴻溝；然而，在 LOD KGs 的背景下，尚未建立對低資源語言的明確定量定義。在這個海報中，我們提出了一種方法來分析 LOD KGs 中語言的分佈，並基於 DBpedia、BabelNet 和 Wikidata 提出了一個初步的多層次分類。這一分類被用來為低資源、高資源和中資源語言提供正式定義，這些定義可用於選擇跨語言轉移的候選者。
2605.05929.author: Ndeye-Emilie Mbengue et.al.
2605.05929.authors: Ndeye-Emilie Mbengue, Pierre Monnin, Miguel Couceiro, Fabien Gandon
